message:
REVIEW: reject extent tree headers claiming depth > ext4 spec limit

`validate_header` previously only bounded `entries` and `max_entries`;
nothing rejected an on-disk root whose `depth` field exceeded the ext4
format limit of 5. Because `walk_subtree`, `descend_search`,
`insert_descend`, and `delete_range_subtree` all recurse on
`header.depth - 1` and trust the parent depth, a corrupt or malicious
image claiming depth up to `u16::MAX` could drive 65535-level recursion
through the tree-traversal helpers (bounded in practice only by the
first read_block failure on a missing child).

The `>= 5` guard at `grow_tree_root` already enforces the limit on the
write/grow path, so the fix is a symmetric rejection on the read path:
add `EXT4_MAX_EXTENT_DEPTH = 5` and have `validate_header` return
`FfsError::Corruption` for any header with `depth > 5`. That covers
every recursive entry point because each of them calls `validate_header`
on the node before descending.

Tests (ffs-btree): 123 passed, 0 failed. New test
`validate_header_depth_exceeds_ext4_limit_is_corruption` covers
depth=6, depth=u16::MAX, and asserts the spec-limit depth=5 still
validates. `ffs-extent` (116) and `ffs-core --lib` (632) regression
suites also green.

Defense-in-depth; no in-tree caller ever passes depth > 5, so no
behavioral regression for correct images.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
